text: Herein, we develop an efficient and highly atom-economical approach to produce benzoxepines by using a rhodium(III) catalyzed terminal C–H functionalization of readily available *ortho*-vinylphenols. A formal (5+2) cycloaddition of these substrates toward various alkynes proceeds smoothly under optimized rhodium catalysis using the precatalyst [Cp*RhCl2]₂ in combination with Cu(OAc)₂ as oxidant in acetonitrile and gives the expected 7-ring oxepine core containing many bioactive natural products in excellent regioselectivity that endures for a wide range of functional groups. More importantly, using CO as alkyne analogue enables for the formal (5+1) cycloaddition toward forming the core structure of biologically relevant coumarins. We deduced the catalytic mechanism through competitions studies using non-conjugated substrate derivatives along with coordinating sites such as phenol. Our experimental evidence reveals a potential catalytic pathway initiating by the coordination of substrate phenol to RhIII catalyst and involves subsequent unique C–H activation assisted potentially through a dearomatization/aromatization process.
